commentary:
# Cross-Exchange Liquidity Commentary – 16 July 2026

US equities command the liquidity hierarchy: Nasdaq 100 and NYSE lead decisively with ILLIQ costs under 0.0056% per $1M USD traded and comparable volumes exceeding $233M USD, though Nasdaq's 0.7646% AR spread trails NYSE's tighter 0.6168% spread. The mid-tier Asian and European bloc—Tokyo Stock Exchange, Euronext, Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and SIX Swiss Exchange—clusters in the 0.0284% to 0.0420% per $1M ILLIQ range, but reveals notable divergence: Tokyo's elevated 2.9217% turnover ratio suggests concentrated, active trading despite modest comparable volume of $49.3M USD, while SIX's remarkably tight 0.4997% AR spread masks structural illiquidity, with just $3.7M USD comparable volume and 0.1839% turnover. Korea Exchange and London Stock Exchange form the laggard tier, with Korea's severe 0.1333% per $1M ILLIQ cost and 1.3963% AR spread signaling material execution friction, and London trailing at 0.0720% per $1M ILLIQ despite an underwhelming $8.1M USD comparable volume. Xetra occupies a precarious middle ground, matching Swiss and Euronext spreads yet posting the lowest turnover ratio at 0.3159%.
